# ctx

> ctx is a queryable world model of a codebase, built to ground and govern the language models that
> modify it. A fast, local Rust CLI, it indexes a repo into a structured, queryable model (symbols,
> call graphs, relationships, semantics), then uses it to (1) GROUND an agent — feed it accurate,
> token-budgeted context selected by meaning and call-graph relevance ("read the right ~2,000 lines,
> not the wrong 200,000"), and (2) GOVERN what the agent changes — impact analysis for the blast
> radius of an edit, plus quality/complexity/duplication gates for CI. Exposed over an MCP server.

ctx works out of the box: `ctx index` builds the world model, then `smart`/`semantic`/`query` ground
the agent and `query impact`/`check`/`score` govern its changes. `snapshot` plus read-only SQL adds a
longitudinal view: whether complexity, coupling, duplication, hotspots, and architecture violations
are improving or deteriorating across commits. Metrics are evidence to investigate, not automatic
proof that code is wrong. Install with `cargo install agentis-ctx`
(the binary is `ctx`). It is not a file-packer (repomix, gitingest) and not an IDE indexer (ctags,
LSP) — it is a code world model, agent-first and local. Links below point to the markdown sources.

## Getting started

- [Introduction](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/intro.md): what ctx is, in one screen
- [Why ctx](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/why-ctx.md): the problem it solves and the approach
- [Getting started](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/getting-started.md): install and first context
- [Comparison](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/comparison.md): ctx vs packers and IDE indexers

## Guides

- [Index & embed first](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/guides/indexing.md): build the index (and embeddings) before anything else; run as background watchers
- [Using ctx with agents](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/guides/using-ctx-with-agents.md): the index → embed → query loop, MCP, and JSON output
- [Context generation](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/context-generation.md): file selection, formats, ignores, token budgeting
- [Code intelligence](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/code-intelligence.md): indexing, search, callers/deps, impact
- [Smart context](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/commands/smart.md): task-based file selection
- [Diff & PR context](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/commands/diff.md): context for git changes
- [Code quality audit](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/commands/audit.md): complexity, duplication, CI gates
- [Interactive shell](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/commands/shell.md): REPL for exploration
- [Map the codebase](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/commands/map.md): token-budgeted structural overview
- [Find existing code](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/commands/similar.md): pre-write reuse check

## Cookbook

- [Cookbook](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/cookbook/index.md): outcome-driven workflows, interpretation principles, and point-in-time versus longitudinal routing
- [Build a codebase health timeline in CI](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/cookbook/continuous-health.md): capture default-branch snapshots, query normalized trends, investigate the responsible code, and see a real ctx-on-ctx case study
- [Govern a pull request without inheriting old debt](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/cookbook/pr-governance.md): compare against the merge base, separate evidence from blocking policy, investigate responsible code, and safely publish analysis for fork pull requests
- [Detect architectural drift before it becomes a rewrite](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/cookbook/architecture-drift.md): define reviewed boundaries, correlate coupling and violation trends, and distinguish code drift from policy-coverage changes
- [Investigate chronic hotspots without chasing large files](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/cookbook/chronic-hotspots.md): combine churn and complexity, confirm persistent pressure, inspect ownership, and avoid score-driven decomposition
- [Review and document intentional complexity](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/cookbook/intentional-complexity.md): distinguish popularity, orchestration, and exhaustive traversal from accidental responsibility before proposing decomposition
- [Track duplication trajectories without forcing abstractions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/cookbook/duplication-trajectories.md): distinguish current and newly introduced pairs, normalize history by repository size, and inspect ownership before extracting reuse
- [Produce a release health report that supports decisions](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/cookbook/release-health-report.md): compare immutable releases with provenance, normalized metrics, investigated findings, policy coverage, uncertainty, and assigned actions
- [Understand an unfamiliar codebase before editing it](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/cookbook/unfamiliar-codebase.md): distinguish structural leads from real entry points, verify one execution path, and report static-analysis uncertainty before editing
- [Build the smallest useful context for a task](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/cookbook/smallest-useful-context.md): rank candidates, audit omitted contracts, use source snippets for investigation, and count the explicit implementation set before packaging it
- [Find existing implementations before writing new code](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/cookbook/find-existing-implementations.md): combine behavior-specific, keyword, and signature-like searches with caller and test inspection before choosing a reuse boundary
- [Trace the blast radius before editing a symbol](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/cookbook/blast-radius.md): bound graph expansion, reject name-resolution false positives from source, and include tests, persisted formats, generated artifacts, public APIs, and external uncertainty
- [Implement a feature with an evidence-backed working set](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/cookbook/evidence-backed-implementation.md): baseline the owner boundary, test the compiled interface, refresh generated contracts and the index, audit context omissions, and interpret before-and-after metrics rather than treating them as verdicts
- [Debug a failing test with a focused evidence loop](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/cookbook/debug-failing-test.md): reproduce the exact symptom, trace test dependencies into the smallest production path, compare neighboring behavior, and prove one causal correction with widening tests
- [Review a large branch without reading every file equally](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/cookbook/review-large-branch.md): inventory every changed path, split the branch into review streams, route attention with scoped metrics, reject false graph expansion, and verify intent against enforcement and CI wiring
- [Downloadable ctx agent skill](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/skills/ctx/SKILL.md): procedural routing and safeguards for agents using ctx to explore, govern, and analyze codebase health

## Governance

- [Quality gates overview](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/integrations/quality-gates.md): the suite, exit-code contract, CI + agent usage
- [Architecture rules (ctx check)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/commands/check.md): rules over .ctx/rules.toml
- [Quality score (ctx score)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/commands/score.md): composite --fail-on gate
- [Hotspots](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/commands/hotspots.md): churn × complexity ranking
- [Duplicates](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/commands/duplicates.md): MinHash near-duplicate detection
- [SQL gates (ctx sql)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/commands/sql.md): raw read-only SQL over the v1 schema, with `--fail-on-rows` for repo-committed gates
- [Historical snapshots](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/commands/snapshot.md): per-commit Parquet metrics, backfills, data-branch CI, and trend queries

## Reference

- [Configuration](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/configuration.md): .contextignore and settings
- [Language support](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/language-support.md): what is extracted per language
- [MCP server](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/commands/serve.md): the eight agent tools
- [JSON output](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/json-output.md): the --json envelope and per-command payloads
- [Exit codes](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/reference/exit-codes.md): the 0/1/2 gate contract

## Integrations

- [Claude / MCP](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/integrations/claude.md): Claude Desktop setup
- [CI/CD](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/integrations/ci-cd.md): GitHub Actions, GitLab, pre-commit
- [VS Code](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/integrations/vscode.md): editor tasks

## Optional

- [Architecture](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/docs/website/docs/architecture.md): how ctx works internally
- [Changelog](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/agentis-tools/ctx/main/CHANGELOG.md): release history
